rationale: The citing paper uses Lim, Ahearne, and Ham (2009) as more than background: it is cited as prior experimental evidence on sales contest prize structure and specifically to motivate hypothesis H3 that lower reward spread can raise effort. The paper then interprets its own reward-spread results in light of that prior finding, while adding disclosure schemes and rank-based utility decomposition. This shows a meaningful functional role in framing and hypothesis development, not just a passing literature-list mention.
relation_description: The 2019 paper builds on Lim et al. (2009)'s prize-structure findings in sales contests and extends that line by introducing disclosure schemes and separating self-generated versus peer-induced rank utility. It uses the earlier empirical result on reward spread as a basis for predictions and comparative interpretation.
